vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of severe degradation. P1 (Yellow Pot) is devoid of visible foliage. P2/P4 (Black Pot) contains a single, struggling stem. P3 (Black Pot) shows the white rabbit anchor, but the Pothos is largely absent or obscured by extreme shadow/necrosis.

plant_audit.P1: No biological activity detected. Soil appears dry and inert.
plant_audit.P2_P4: Single thin, chlorotic stem remains. No healthy leaf tissue visible.
plant_audit.P3: Rabbit anchor present; plant material is non-viable or missing.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears compacted and dry across all vessels.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments present in P3/P4 area; likely an attempt at calcium supplementation or pest deterrent, currently ineffective.
biome_observations.fungal_presence: None detected, likely due to extreme desiccation.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-1: Progressive loss of turgor pressure in the remaining stem. The stem in the black pot has shifted orientation, indicating structural collapse.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Total loss of visual definition. The environment has transitioned to a state of near-total darkness or complete plant senescence.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is effectively dead. The lack of chlorophyll-rich tissue and the collapse of the primary stem structure indicate a terminal state of neglect or environmental failure.
vision_report.anomalies: Presence of eggshell fragments (Intervention). The 'White Rabbit' anchor is the only stable element in a failing system.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical collection has suffered a catastrophic decline. Over the observed period, the specimens have transitioned from a state of visible, albeit sparse, life to complete senescence. The soil is devoid of moisture, and the lack of light/maintenance has resulted in the total loss of the registered species. The environment is currently non-viable for plant life.
